channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Trump opens G7 talks hailing Iran deal, signals push to end Ukraine war
item.description: 🇫🇷 President Trump arrived at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, touting the newly signed US-Iran agreement and telling allies the deal will bring success for the world, while signaling he now wants to turn his attention to ending the Ukraine-Russia war. (Source: AP News) [Diplomacy]

item.title: US and Iran announce deal to end war and reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 The US and Iran have reached an agreement to end military operations and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a breakthrough that could reshape the Middle East and ease global oil supply fears. However, key details about enforcement and timelines remain unresolved. (Source: NPR) [US-Iran War]

item.title: France and Britain push multinational Hormuz naval mission as ceasefire nears
item.description: 🛢️ France and Britain are advancing plans for a multinational naval mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz if a US-Iran ceasefire holds, though it remains unclear whether Tehran would accept any such foreign naval presence. (Source: Reuters) [Military]

item.title: Russia slashes fuel quality standards as Ukrainian drone strikes squeeze refineries
item.description: 🇷🇺 Russia is allowing refineries to produce fuel with lower environmental specifications for the domestic market, Kommersant reports, as Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure have doubled since the start of 2026, forcing partial shutdowns and supply squeezes. (Source: Reuters) [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: US-Iran deal reached but key questions remain as Trump arrives at G7
item.description: ⚔️ The US and Iran say they have reached an agreement set to take effect Friday, but critical questions about enforcement, monitoring, and the fate of regional allies remain unanswered. The breakthrough came just hours before Trump landed in France for the G7 summit. (Source: CNN) [US-Iran War]

item.title: Erdogan Says Turkey Chose Diplomacy Over Escalation in Iran Conflict
item.description: 🇹🇷 President Erdogan stated Turkey pursued a measured, diplomacy-first policy during the US-Iran conflict, positioning Turkey as a regional mediator as the Iran deal takes shape. (Source: Daily Sabah) [Diplomacy]

item.title: Israeli Defense Firms Booths Boarded Up at Eurosatory After French Ban
item.description: 🇫🇷🇮🇱 France shut down Israeli exhibitor spaces at the Eurosatory defense trade show, boarding up booths of smaller Israeli firms while larger companies remain stripped of weapons displays, drawing sharp criticism from Israel’s Defense Ministry. (Source: Breaking Defense) [Defense Industry]

item.title: Trump, Vance, and Iran Speaker Ghalibaf Electronically Sign Peace Memo
item.description: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 A senior US official confirmed that President Trump, Vice President Vance, and Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf electronically signed the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with a formal ceremony planned for Friday in Geneva. (Source: Al Arabiya) [Diplomacy]

item.title: G7 Allies Seek Clarity on Iran Deal as Trump Arrives in France
item.description: 🌍 World leaders gathering at the G7 summit in France pressed for details on the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with questions remaining about enforcement, the Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline, and Israel-Hezbollah tensions that could threaten the deal. (Source: TRT World) [Geopolitics]

item.title: G7 Summit Opens in France as Trump Arrives With Iran Deal and Tariff Threats
item.description: 🇫🇷 World leaders gathered at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, where relief over the US-Iran peace deal was tempered by Trump’s new 100% tariff threats against French wines over a digital services tax. (Source: Reuters) [Diplomacy]

item.title: Israelis Across Political Spectrum Furious Over US-Iran Deal
item.description: 🇮🇱 Israelis from across the political spectrum are expressing anger at the interim US-Iran peace deal, calling it a disaster and putting pressure on Netanyahu as Israel vows to keep forces in Lebanon and Syria. (Source: AP News) [Middle East]

item.title: Somaliland Opens Embassy in Jerusalem After Israeli Recognition
item.description: 🇸🇴 Somaliland opened its embassy in Jerusalem, months after Israel became the first country to recognize the breakaway African state, in a move with significant geopolitical implications for the Horn of Africa. (Source: Asharq Al-Awsat) [Diplomacy]

item.title: Global Stocks Surge and Oil Prices Drop After US-Iran Deal
item.description: 📈 Shares soared worldwide and oil prices fell after the US and Iran reached a tentative deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, though analysts warn it could take weeks or months for oil flows to normalize. (Source: AP News) [Economy]

item.title: US-Iran deal signed as Trump and Iranian negotiator finalize agreement, but Israel vows to stay in Lebanon
item.description: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 The US and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel immediately declared it would not withdraw from southern Lebanon, while Iran insisted on receiving half of its frozen assets before final negotiations. (Source: CBS News) [US-Iran War]

item.title: Ukraine officially launches EU membership negotiations amid ongoing war with Russia
item.description: 🇺🇦🇪🇺 Ukraine began formal EU accession talks on Monday, starting a years-long process of reforms even as it continues fighting Russia's invasion. The move marks a historic shift for Kyiv's geopolitical orientation. (Source: AP News) [Europe]

item.title: EU Adopts New Russia Sanctions Targeting Energy Revenues and Propaganda
item.description: 🇪🇶⚔️ The Council of the EU approved a new sanctions package against Russia, adding 34 individuals and 47 entities, with a focus on energy revenue flows and state propaganda infrastructure supporting the invasion of Ukraine. (Source: Kyiv Independent) [Europe]

item.title: 4 Rescuers Killed in Russian Double-Tap Strike on Kharkiv
item.description: 🇺🇦💔 Russian forces carried out a double-tap missile strike on Kharkiv, killing four rescue workers and injuring six others. The second missile hit approximately 30 seconds after the first, targeting emergency responders arriving at the scene. (Source: Kyiv Independent) [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: Economist Analysis: Ukraine Intensifying Air War Reflects Russia Ground Difficulties
item.description: 🇺🇦✈️ The Economist reports that the escalating air war with mass drone and missile barrages is partly a reflection of Russia's increasing difficulties on the ground, as Ukraine adapts with new drone tactics and technology. (Source: The Economist) [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: EU Sanctions Putins Confessor and Russias Prosecutor General in New Package
item.description: 🇪🇶🏛️ The EU added 34 individuals and 47 organizations to its sanctions list, including Putin's personal confessor and Russia's Prosecutor General, expanding pressure on Moscow's inner circle and propaganda apparatus. (Source: Meduza) [Europe]

item.title: Israel vows to stay 'indefinitely' in lands seized in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza
item.description: 🇮🇱⚔️ Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said ground forces will remain indefinitely in territories captured in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza, potentially challenging the US-Iran ceasefire deal that hinges on regional de-escalation. (Source: France24) [Middle East]

item.title: US-Iran Deal Faces Scrutiny as Key Questions Remain
item.description: 🇮🇷 The preliminary US-Iran ceasefire agreement, announced hours before the G7 summit, leaves major unresolved issues including Iran's nuclear program, enforcement mechanisms, and the timeline for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: CNN) [US-Iran War]

item.title: Russian Strike Sets Fire to Historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra Cathedral
item.description: 🇺🇦 A massive overnight Russian drone and missile barrage on Kyiv killed at least 11 people and set fire to the 1,000-year-old Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery. Russia denied striking the cathedral, blaming Ukrainian air defense systems. (Source: Reuters) [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: London court convicts two men in Russian-linked plot to firebomb properties tied to UK Prime Minister
item.description: 🇬🇧 Two men were convicted of an arson plot orchestrated by a mysterious Russian-speaking figure known as "El Money" who offered payment via Telegram to torch houses and a car linked to Keir Starmer. (Source: SCMP) [Europe]

item.title: Drone strikes killed over 1,000 civilians in Sudan in first five months of 2026, UN rights chief says
item.description: 🇸🇩 A senior UN official reported that drone strikes have killed more than 1,000 civilians in war-torn Sudan since January, highlighting the devastating toll of the ongoing civil conflict between the SAF and RSF. (Source: The Hill) [Africa]

item.title: Chinese analysts flag Israel as wildcard in US-Iran deal, warn agreement may be messy and short-lived
item.description: 🇨🇳 As the US, Iran and Pakistan announced a peace agreement, Chinese analysts cautioned that Israel remains the biggest unpredictable factor and the deal could unravel if core issues are left unresolved. (Source: SCMP) [Diplomacy]
